metadata.title: Documented Historical Sundown Towns
metadata.description: Geocoded sundown towns from the Scientific Data (2025) dataset, linked to Census 2020 geographies via GJOIN codes and Census Gazetteer files.

source.paper: Nardos, Rahel, et al. "A national data set of historical US sundown towns for quantitative analysis." Scientific Data 12 (2025).
source.coordinates_source: US Census Bureau 2020 Gazetteer Files (place, county, county subdivision)
source.license: CC-BY (Scientific Data dataset), Public Domain (Census gazetteer)

evidence_tiers.Confirmed: Designated "Surely" in source dataset. Strong primary source documentation.
evidence_tiers.Probable: Designated "Probable" in source dataset. Multiple secondary sources, consistent historical record.
evidence_tiers.Possible: Designated "Possible" in source dataset. Limited documentation, demographic evidence only.

metadata.incompleteness_note: This dataset documents 2,248 sundown places. Researchers estimate the actual total was far higher. Illinois alone may have had 500+ sundown towns (70% of all towns). What is shown is the documented floor, not the ceiling.
metadata.pipeline: Joined OSF dataset (sundown_linked_to_census.csv) to Census 2020 Gazetteer place/county/cousub files via GJOIN2020 codes and centroid2020 fallback.
